qna_pairs.q: How exactly does the ve(3,3) model work? I've heard of ve(3,3) but don't fully get the mechanics.
qna_pairs.a: You lock MARB tokens to get veTokens, which give you voting power. Longer locks = more voting power + more rewards. Each week, veHolders vote on which liquidity pools get MARB emissions. Projects can offer bribes to attract votes to their pools.

qna_pairs.q: What's the benefit for regular users who don't want to lock their tokens?
qna_pairs.a: Even if you don't lock MARB, you can still farm in the liquidity pools that veHolders vote to receive emissions. The system rewards long-term lockers while still offering earning opportunities for all participants.

qna_pairs.q: How will you handle the initial liquidity when there's no presale or VC backing?
qna_pairs.a: Fair question. The community launch means liquidity comes from users who believe in the model. We're focusing on attracting projects that want to offer bribes to get their pools emissions, which will naturally draw liquidity providers.

qna_pairs.q: What makes MegaETH a good choice for this veDEX compared to other chains?
qna_pairs.a: MegaETH offers low fees and a growing user base. Being the first veDEX on MegaETH gives us a first-mover advantage in this ecosystem, and the community there seems ready for innovative DEX models.

qna_pairs.q: How do you prevent whales from dominating the voting system?
qna_pairs.a: The ve(3,3) model already encourages longer locks rather than bigger stakes, which distributes power. We'll also monitor voting patterns and may implement reasonable caps on individual voting power if needed to maintain decentralization.

qna_pairs.q: How is MarbMarket different from other DEXs that claim to be community-focused?
qna_pairs.a: MarbMarket is the first veDEX on MegaETH with a true ve(3,3) model. Unlike others, we have no presale or VC backing - just users locking MARB for voting power and directing emissions. It's pure community control from day one.

qna_pairs.q: What happens if projects just stop paying bribes? Won't that kill the incentive loop?
qna_pairs.a: That's a fair concern. The system works best when projects value the liquidity our voters direct. We're starting with strong launch partners committed to the model, and the longer locks give early users more influence to keep incentives flowing.

qna_pairs.q: If I lock MARB tokens, how long do they need to stay locked to earn maximum voting power?
qna_pairs.a: The ve(3,3) model rewards longer locks with more influence. Maximum voting power comes from locking MARB for the full duration, which is 4 years. The longer you lock, the more voting power and rewards you earn.

qna_pairs.q: How does a 'fair launch' actually work in practice? What prevents insiders from getting special treatment?
qna_pairs.a: Our contract was deployed with no owner privileges. The team has no special access or tokens beyond what's available publicly. Everyone starts with equal opportunity to lock MARB and participate in governance from day one.

qna_pairs.q: Can you explain the incentive loop again? How does actually money flow from projects back to token holders?
qna_pairs.a: Projects pay bribes in MARB to veHolders who vote for their pools. These bribes go directly to the locker's wallets. The more emissions a pool gets, the more LPs earn, which attracts more liquidity - all directed by the community through votes.

content:
Been seeing a lot of noise about new DEX launches lately. Most miss the point. It's not about fees. It's about who controls the incentives. That's why MarbMarket caught my eye. First veDEX on MegaETH with no presale, no VCs. Just pure community power.

The ve(3,3) model is simple yet brilliant. Lock MARB, get voting power, direct emissions to the pools you want. Longer locks mean more influence. Projects pay bribes for those votes. The incentives flow back to the lockers. It's a self-reinforcing loop that rewards early believers.

Fair launch means no one gets special treatment. No insiders. Just users who believe in the system. I've seen too many launches where the deck was stacked from day one. This feels different. @RallyOnChain

Check it out yourself. x.com/Marb_market

How do we prevent the ve(3,3) model from becoming just another governance capture mechanism over time?
